content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 25, 1988 
Isabelle B. Gonthier 
Rio Hondo community College District 
3600 Workman Mill Road 
Whittier, CA 
90608 
Dear Ms. Gonthier: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-88-122 
You have written for advice concerning various actions of 
the Rio Hondo Community College District. 
The Fair Political Commission is responsible for 
interpretin1 and enforcing the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act.li 
Your concerns are not addressed by the Act. 
Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on this subject. 
You might contact your local district attorney to discuss the 
Brown Act violations you believe have occurred. 
We appreciate your interest in the commission. 
If we may 
be of assistance in the future, please let me know. 
DMG:plh 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. 
General Counsel 
11 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


RIO HOl'tPO ,COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT 
3600 Workman Mill R6ad • Whittier, CA 90608 • Phone 692-0921 
March 8, 1988 
Fair Political Practice Committee 
Mr. John Larson, Chair 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Sir: 
Office of the President 
This is a formal request to investigate and/or advise me on a matter of grave 
importance. 
My concern centers around the actions of the Board of Trustees of 
the Rio Hondo Community College District of which I am a member. 
At an 
official Board meeting on February 24, 1988, I presented the following 
complaints: 
1. 
That action is taken - orders given to administration to produce 
something without benefit of motion, second, or vote. 
That these 
actions were not on the agenda for consideration by all Board 
Members. 
2. 
That individual administrators are given orders to perform tasks for 
individual Board Members without consent or knowledge of the entire 
Board. 
That the information is acquired and Board Members are not 
made aware of its intention or use. 
3. 
That staff is assigned/appointed to sit on committees l>lithout 
benefit of full Board approval - motion, second, and vote. 
4. 
That committees are established and Board Members appointed to that 
committee again without full Board approval - motion, second, and 
vote. 
5. 
That three Board Members are seen meeting together after the Board 
Meetings, alone and with staff members, which is a violation of the 
Brown Act. 
6. 
That staff members sit in the audience and are seen signaling Board 
Members, with thumbs up or the finger sign for okay. 
7. 
That staff members are allowed to come to an open forum to criticize 
their superiors with what appears to be approval by some Board 
Members. 
Board 
Trustees: ",aucuc; B. Gonthier 411 
E. Hernandez .. Marilee Morgan. Ralph S. Pacheco • Hilda Solis 


Mr. John Larson 
March 8, 1988 
Page 2 
8. 
That as an entire Board, we are not communicating - we are not being 
fully informed. 
Our rights to full consideration along with the 
opportunity of due deliberation on impending issues, actions, and 
transactions are being usurped and thereby depriving some Board 
Members and their constituents of their rights to equal governance 
of the college. 
Because the above incidences are occurring more frequently and there seems to 
be no way to correct them, I am appealing to sources outside the college for 
assistance. 
I hope we can avoid the negative impact that improper 
boardsmanship will surely have on our 1989 application for reaccreditation. 
I 
cannot remain silent as the participation in governance of the college is 
being taken from me and the public constituency that elected me. 
If you have any further questions, you can call me at (work) 213/698-2278, or 
(home) 213/693-9343. 
I anxiously await your response. 
Isabelle B. Gonthier 
Clerk, Board of Trustees 
Rio Hondo Community College District 
IBG:ss 
cc:Whittier Daily News 
Whittier Highlander 
Los Angeles Times, San Gabriel Section 
Los Angeles Times, Southeast Section 
Mid Valley News 
La Habra Review 
Pico Rivera News 
Quad Shopper 
RHC Board of Trustees

sections.question: You have written for advice concerning various actions of the Rio Hondo Community College District. [The requestor raised 8 specific complaints including: actions taken without proper Board procedures (motion, second, vote), administrators given orders by individual Board Members, staff assigned to committees without full Board approval, committees established without full Board approval, three Board Members meeting together in violation of the Brown Act, staff members signaling Board Members, staff members criticizing superiors with apparent Board approval, and the entire Board not communicating or being fully informed regarding governance matters.]
sections.conclusion: Your concerns are not addressed by the Act. Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on this subject. You might contact your local district attorney to discuss the Brown Act violations you believe have occurred.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor, a Board member of Rio Hondo Community College District, seeks FPPC advice on whether various actions of the Board of Trustees—including actions taken without proper procedural votes, unauthorized private meetings among Board members, and alleged Brown Act violations—constitute violations of political reform laws.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the described actions fall outside the scope of the Political Reform Act and therefore cannot provide advice on these matters, instead referring the requestor to the local district attorney regarding potential Brown Act violations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated March 8, 1988) and the FPPC response letter (dated March 25, 1988). The response uses a declination format rather than a full Q/A format. The substantive concerns raised relate primarily to Brown Act violations rather than Political Reform Act issues.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have written for advice concerning various actions of the Rio Hondo Community College District. [The requestor raised 8 specific complaints including: actions taken without proper Board procedures (motion, second, vote), administrators given orders by individual Board Members, staff assigned to committees without full Board approval, committees established without full Board approval, three Board Members meeting together in violation of the Brown Act, staff members signaling Board Members, staff members criticizing superiors with apparent Board approval, and the entire Board not communicating or being fully informed regarding governance matters.]

CONCLUSION: Your concerns are not addressed by the Act. Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on this subject. You might contact your local district attorney to discuss the Brown Act violations you believe have occurred.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice on a Rio Hondo Community College District Board member's complaints about Board governance procedures and alleged Brown Act violations, finding the issues fall outside the Political Reform Act's jurisdiction and referring the matter to the local district attorney.
